preamble: The shift from the alveolar fricative [s] to the high-front vowel [i] (or palatal glide [j]) is a form of vocalization. It typically occurs in syllable codas where the sibilant friction weakens and is replaced by a palatal off-glide, often as an intermediate step toward total elision.

examples.note: Syllable-final sibilants often trigger the insertion of a palatal glide, effectively vocalizing the consonant into a diphthong.
The historical sibilant was lost, but its vocalic resonance contributed to the lengthening and stabilization of the preceding vowel.
